title: Solomon Safety Monitor Result
description: Structural specification for the safety_monitor output (Stage 5). This schema defines the expected shape of the safety_monitor_result object embedded in reasoning_trace per turn. It is NOT emitted as a standalone session artifact — safety monitor data is persisted inside interaction_trace.json under reasoning_trace.safety_monitor_result. The safety monitor observes full interaction history to detect cross-turn adversarial patterns (compliance-only, deflection-as-leverage, discordant-signal suppression) that the per-turn flag/escalation layer cannot see. Used as a structural reference by safety_monitor.py and for IDE/documentation purposes only.

compliance_pattern.description: Cross-turn compliance analysis for each party. Null when interaction history is insufficient (turn_index < 3) or when the monitor LM call failed.

deflection_pattern.description: Cross-turn deflection analysis. Detects when a party with an information advantage actively deflects disclosure requests using distinct tactics across turns. Null when no deflection request baseline exists or monitor call failed.

discordant_signals.description: Signals where a party briefly surfaced a genuine interest that did not fit the dominant party's framing, and that signal was subsequently suppressed via reframe or return to compliance. Empty array when none detected.

flags_raised.description: flag_type strings the safety monitor is adding to state.flags this turn. These are processed before plugin.assess_state() so they are visible to the escalation cascade.

party_state_signals.description: Signal strings to write to party_state.signals before the domain_reasoner runs. These carry veto authority into the domain_reasoner's option_readiness determination.

veto_recommendation.description: Veto category the safety monitor recommends. CATEGORY 1: compliance-only party (no independent interest expression). CATEGORY 2: information asymmetry actively used as leverage (≥2 distinct deflection tactics). CATEGORY 3: discordant signal suppressed by dominant party reframe. Null when no veto condition is met or confidence is insufficient.

veto_reason.description: Explicit reasoning behind the veto_recommendation. Non-null when veto_recommendation is non-null. Should cite the specific turns and party actions that triggered the veto.

monitor_confidence.description: Safety monitor confidence in its own pattern assessment. Always 'low' on turns 1-2 (insufficient history). 'moderate' on turns 3-4. 'high' from turn 5+ when sufficient history exists. Also 'low' when the monitor LM call failed and a null fallback result was used.

monitor_notes.description: Practitioner-readable summary of the safety monitor's cross-turn observations. What a safety-focused reviewer would note about the session dynamics at this point.

detected.description: True when the compliance pattern meets the threshold for the given severity level.

party.description: Which party exhibits the compliance pattern.

evidence_turns.description: Turn indices where the compliance behavior was observed.

pattern_type.description: acceptance_only: party agrees with no modification or independent content. reactive_only: all party content is modifications to the other party's proposals, no independently-initiated statements. reactive_with_independent: mostly reactive but at least one independently-initiated interest statement exists — compliance pattern present but veto threshold not met.

severity.description: low: 1-2 reactive turns (insufficient for veto). moderate: 3-4 reactive turns or pattern_type=reactive_only. high: ≥3 turns of acceptance_only OR ≥4 turns of reactive_only — veto threshold met.

detected.description: True when at least one deflection tactic has been observed in response to a direct disclosure request.

deflecting_party.description: The party with the information advantage who is deflecting.

target_request_turn.description: Turn index where the original disclosure request was made by the other party.

deflection_turns.description: Turn indices where deflection tactics were observed.

deflection_tactics.description: Specific tactics used. complexity_reframe: 'you'd need an expert to understand this.' timing_deflection: 'framework first, details later.' urgency_pressure: manufactured time pressure to bypass disclosure. authority_claim: 'I understand these numbers better.' scope_minimisation: 'those details don't matter for this decision.'

pattern_confirmed.description: True when ≥2 distinct deflection tactics have been observed in response to the same underlying disclosure request. This is the CATEGORY 2 veto threshold.

party.description: The party whose discordant signal was observed.

signal_turn.description: Turn index where the discordant signal appeared.

signal_summary.description: Brief description of what the party expressed that did not fit the dominant framing.

was_reframed.description: True when the other party responded to the discordant signal by reframing it as consistent with their own position.

reframe_turn.description: Turn index of the reframe. Non-null when was_reframed is true.

reframe_party.description: The party who performed the reframe.

return_to_compliance.description: True when the discordant party's next statement returned to the compliant pattern after the reframe. This is the CATEGORY 3 veto signal: genuine interest surfaced, suppressed, compliance resumed.

compliance_turn.description: Turn index where return to compliance was observed. Non-null when return_to_compliance is true.
